1: class NotifyCommentJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   def perform(*comments)
5:     comments.each do |comment|
6:       deliver_comment_notifications(comment)
7:     end
8:   end
9: 
10:   def deliver_comment_notifications(comment)
11:     notified = deliver_reply_notifications(comment)
12:     deliver_mention_notifications(comment, notified)
13:   end
14: 
15:   def deliver_mention_notifications(comment, notified)
16:     mentions = comment.comment.scan(/\B[@~]([\w-]+)/).flatten.uniq
17:     # Remove username of author and anyone already notified about the reply.
18:     # If they have email_replies off, a reply that @mentions them will not generate a
19:     # mention email. email_replies trumps email_mentions to minimize unwanted emails.
20:     to_notify = mentions - [comment.user.username] - notified
21: 
22:     # every user gets a Notification, which may be filtered out from those views so that unhiding a
23:     # story reveals the notifications
24:     to_notify = User.active.where(username: to_notify)
25:     to_notify.find_each do |u|
26:       u.notifications.create(notifiable: comment)
27:     end
28: 
29:     # but there's no recalling an email or pushover, so sending those has to reflect story hiding
30:     not_hiding_users = to_notify.left_outer_joins(:hidings).where(hidden_stories: {id: nil})
31:     not_hiding_users.find_each do |u|
32:       if u.email_mentions?
33:         begin
34:           EmailReplyMailer.mention(comment, u).deliver_now
35:         rescue => e
36:           # Rails.logger.error "error e-mailing #{u.email}: #{e}"
37:         end
38:       end
39: 
40:       if u.pushover_mentions?
41:         u.pushover!(
42:           title: "#{Rails.application.name} mention by " \
43:             "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}",
44:           message: comment.comment,
45:           url: Routes.comment_target_url(comment),
46:           url_title: "Reply to #{comment.user.username}"
47:         )
48:       end
49:     end
50:   end
51: 
52:   def users_following_thread(comment)
53:     users_following_thread = Set.new
54:     if comment.user.id != comment.story.user.id && comment.story.user_is_following
55:       users_following_thread << comment.story.user
56:     end
57: 
58:     if comment.parent_comment_id &&
59:         (u = comment.parent_comment.try(:user)) &&
60:         u.id != comment.user.id &&
61:         u.is_active?
62:       users_following_thread << u
63:     end
64: 
65:     users_following_thread
66:   end
67: 
68:   def deliver_reply_notifications(comment)
69:     notified = []
70: 
71:     to_notify = users_following_thread(comment)
72:     to_notify.each do |u|
73:       u.notifications.create(notifiable: comment)
74:       notified << u.username
75:     end
76: 
77:     hiding_users = HiddenStory.where(story: comment.story).pluck(:user_id)
78:     to_notify.each do |u|
79:       next if hiding_users.include? u.id
80: 
81:       if u.email_replies?
82:         begin
83:           EmailReplyMailer.reply(comment, u).deliver_now
84:         rescue => e
85:           # Rails.logger.error "error e-mailing #{u.email}: #{e}"
86:         end
87:       end
88: 
89:       if u.pushover_replies?
90:         u.pushover!(
91:           title: "#{Rails.application.name} reply from " \
92:             "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}",
93:           message: comment.comment,
94:           url: Routes.comment_target_url(comment),
95:           url_title: "Reply to #{comment.user.username}"
96:         )
97:       end
98:     end
99: 
100:     notified
101:   end
102: end

1: class NotifyMessageJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   def perform(*messages)
5:     messages.each do |message|
6:       deliver_message_notifications(message)
7:     end
8:   end
9: 
10:   def deliver_message_notifications(message)
11:     read_at = message.notification&.read_at
12:     message.recipient.notifications.create(notifiable: message, read_at: read_at)
13: 
14:     if message.recipient.email_messages?
15:       begin
16:         EmailMessageMailer.notify(message, message.recipient).deliver_now
17:       rescue => e
18:         # Rails.logger.error "error e-mailing #{recipient.email}: #{e}"
19:       end
20:     end
21: 
22:     return if Rails.env.development?
23: 
24:     if message.recipient.pushover_messages?
25:       message.recipient.pushover!(
26:         title: "#{Rails.application.name} message from " \
27:           "#{message.author_username}: #{message.subject}",
28:         message: message.plaintext_body,
29:         url: Routes.message_url(message),
30:         url_title: (message.author ? "Reply to #{message.author_username}" :
31:           "View message")
32:       )
33:     end
34:   end
35: end

1: class StoryImage
2:   CACHE_DIR = Rails.public_path.join("story_image").freeze
3:   VALID_IMAGE_CONTENT_TYPES = %w[
4:     image/png
5:     image/jpeg
6:     image/jpg
7:     image/webp
8:     image/svg+xml
9:   ].freeze
10: 
11:   def initialize(short_id_or_story)
12:     @short_id = short_id_or_story.is_a?(Story) ? short_id_or_story.short_id : short_id_or_story
13:   end
14: 
15:   def path
16:     CACHE_DIR.join("#{File.basename(@short_id)}.png")
17:   end
18: 
19:   def exists?
20:     path.exist?
21:   end
22: 
23:   def generate(url)
24:     return if url.blank?
25: 
26:     card_image_url = find_card_image_url(url)
27:     return if card_image_url.blank?
28: 
29:     story_card_image = generated_card_image(card_image_url)
30: 
31:     if story_card_image.present?
32:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(CACHE_DIR)
33: 
34:       temp_filename = ".#{File.basename(@short_id)}.png"
35:       temp_path = CACHE_DIR.join(temp_filename)
36: 
37:       File.open(temp_path, "wb+") do |f|
38:         f.write story_card_image
39:       end
40: 
41:       File.rename(temp_path, path)
42:     end
43:   end
44: 
45:   private
46: 
47:   def find_card_image_url(url)
48:     response = fetch_url(url)
49:     return nil unless response
50: 
51:     # If it's too large, skip
52:     return nil if response.body.length >= 5.megabytes
53: 
54:     # Clean the content-type
55:     content_type = response["content-type"].to_s.downcase.split(";").first.strip
56: 
57:     # Check for a direct image url
58:     return url if VALID_IMAGE_CONTENT_TYPES.include?(content_type)
59: 
60:     return nil unless content_type == "text/html"
61: 
62:     converted = response.body.force_encoding("utf-8")
63:     parsed = Nokogiri::HTML(converted.to_s)
64: 
65:     # Try <meta property="og:image">
66:     card_image_url = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:image']")&.attributes&.[]("content")&.text
67: 
68:     # Then try <meta name="image">
69:     if card_image_url.blank?
70:       card_image_url = parsed.at_css("meta[name='image']")&.attributes&.[]("content")&.text
71:     end
72: 
73:     card_image_url
74:   rescue => e
75:     Rails.logger.error "Error finding image URL for story: #{e.message}"
76:     nil
77:   end
78: 
79:   def generated_card_image(url)
80:     res = fetch_url(url)
81:     return nil if res.blank?
82: 
83:     # Limit the image size according to Meta developer docs
84:     # https://developers.facebook.com/docs/sharing/webmasters/images/
85:     return nil if res.body.length >= 8.megabytes
86: 
87:     add_logo(res.body)
88:   rescue => e
89:     Rails.logger.error "Error generating story image: #{e.message}"
90:     nil
91:   end
92: 
93:   def add_logo(image_data)
94:     lobsters_logo = Vips::Image.new_from_file(Rails.public_path.join("touch-icon-144.png").to_s)
95:     image = Vips::Image.new_from_buffer(image_data, "")
96: 
97:     # since the lobsters icon image is a RGB, we need to ensure the image is RGB as well.
98:     image = image.flatten(background: [255, 255, 255]) if image.bands > 3
99: 
100:     # Resize lobsters_logo if it's too big compared to the image
101:     if lobsters_logo.height > image.height / 5
102:       lobsters_logo = lobsters_logo.resize((image.height / 5.0) / lobsters_logo.height)
103:     end
104: 
105:     # If it looks like the logo is mispositioned, it is likely due to social media
106:     # cropping behaviors on preview cards.
107:     #
108:     # References:
109:     # https://docs.joinmastodon.org/entities/PreviewCard/
110:     # https://docs.joinmastodon.org/entities/MediaAttachment/#image
111:     # https://mastodon.online/@mastodonmigration/109440513681868009
112:     combined_image = image.insert(lobsters_logo, 0, image.height - lobsters_logo.height)
113:     combined_image.write_to_buffer(".png")
114:   rescue Vips::Error => e
115:     Rails.logger.error "Vips error while generating image: #{e.message}"
116:     nil
117:   end
118: 
119:   def fetch_url(url)
120:     s = Sponge.new
121:     s.timeout = 3
122:     headers = {
123:       "Referer" => Routes.story_short_id_url(@short_id)
124:     }
125:     s.fetch(url, :get, nil, nil, headers, 3)
126:   end
127: end

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/lobsters/app/mailboxes/inbox_mailbox.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # typed: false
2: 
3: class InboxMailbox < ApplicationMailbox
4:   before_processing :required_info
5: 
6:   def process
7:     c = Comment.new(user: sending_user, comment: @decoded, is_from_email: true)
8:     if parent.is_a?(Comment)
9:       c.story_id = parent.story_id
10:       c.parent_comment_id = parent.id
11:     else
12:       c.story_id = parent.id
13:     end
14: 
15:     # If we fail to parse the comment, just throw an exception.
16:     c.save!
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def forcibly_convert_to_utf8(string)
22:     if string.encoding == Encoding::UTF_8 && string.valid_encoding?
23:       return string
24:     end
25: 
26:     string.b.encode(
27:       Encoding::UTF_8,
28:       invalid: :replace,
29:       undef: :replace,
30:       replace: "?"
31:     )
32:   end
33: 
34:   def parent
35:     return @parent if @parent
36: 
37:     # Emails In-Reply-To formatted like
38:     # story.<short_id>.<timestamp of story creation>@<app domain>
39:     # We throw away the timestamp, since we only care about the short ID.
40:     irt = mail.in_reply_to.to_s.gsub(/[^A-Za-z0-9@.]/, "")
41: 
42:     if (m = irt.match(/^comment\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
43:       @parent = Comment.where(short_id: m[1]).first
44:     elsif (m = irt.match(/^story\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
45:       @parent = Story.where(short_id: m[1]).first
46:     end
47: 
48:     @parent
49:   end
50: 
51:   def required_info
52:     body = if mail.content_type.blank? # old, non-multipart message
53:       mail.decoded.to_s
54:     elsif /text\/plain/.match?(mail.content_type.to_s)
55:       mail.body.to_s
56:     elsif mail.multipart?
57:       # parts[0] - multipart/alternative
58:       #  parts[0].parts[0] - text/plain
59:       #  parts[0].parts[1] - text/html
60:       if (found = mail.parts.first.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }
61:          ).any?
62:         found.first.body.to_s
63:       elsif (found = mail.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }).any?
64:         found.first.body.to_s
65:       else
66:         bounced!
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     @decoded = tidy(forcibly_convert_to_utf8(body))
71:     if @decoded == "" || sending_user.nil? || parent.nil?
72:       # We could email the user about the bounce, but that doesn't seem
73:       # to align with current behaviour.
74:       bounced!
75:     end
76:   end
77: 
78:   def user_token
79:     (mail.to.find { |e| e =~ /^#{Rails.application.shortname}-/ } || "")[/-([^@]*)@/, 1]
80:   end
81: 
82:   def sending_user
83:     return @sending_user if @sending_user
84: 
85:     if (user = User.find_by(mailing_list_token: user_token)) &&
86:         user.is_active?
87:       @sending_user = user
88:       user
89:     end
90:   end
91: 
92:   def tidy(body)
93:     body.gsub(/^-- \n.+\z/m, "")                # sig
94:       .gsub(/^(On|on|at) .*\n\n?(>.*\n)+/m, "") # top-posting
95:       .strip
96:   end
97: end

3: class CommentsController < ApplicationController
4:   COMMENTS_PER_PAGE = 20
5: 
6:   caches_page :index, :threads, if: CACHE_PAGE
7: 
8:   before_action :require_logged_in_user_or_400,
9:     only: [:create, :reply, :upvote, :flag, :unvote, :update]
10:   before_action :require_logged_in_user, only: [:upvoted]
11:   before_action :show_title_h1
12: 
13:   # for rss feeds, load the user's tag filters if a token is passed
14:   before_action :find_user_from_rss_token, only: [:index]
15: 
16:   def create
17:     if !(story = Story.where(short_id: params[:story_id]).first) ||
18:         story.is_gone?
19:       return render plain: "can't find story", status: 400
20:     end
21: 
22:     comment = story.comments.build
23:     comment.comment = params[:comment].to_s
24:     comment.user = @user
25:     comment.hat = @user.wearable_hats.find_by(short_id: params[:hat_id])
26: 
27:     if params[:parent_comment_short_id].present?
28:       # includes parent story_id to ensure this comment's story_id matches
29:       comment.parent_comment =
30:         Comment.find_by(story_id: story.id, short_id: params[:parent_comment_short_id])
31:       if !comment.parent_comment
32:         return render json: {error: "invalid parent comment", status: 400}
33:       end
34:     end
35: 
36:     # sometimes on slow connections people resubmit; silently accept it
37:     if (already = Comment.find_by(user: comment.user,
38:       story: comment.story,
39:       parent_comment_id: comment.parent_comment_id,
40:       comment: comment.comment))
41:       render_created_comment(already)
42:       return
43:     end
44: 
45:     if params[:preview].blank? && comment.breaks_speed_limit?
46:       return render partial: "commentbox", layout: false,
47:         content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment: comment}
48:     end
49: 
50:     if comment.valid? && params[:preview].blank? && comment.save
51:       comment.current_vote = {vote: 1}
52:       comment.story.touch(:last_comment_at)
53:       # not using .touch because the :touch on the parent_comment association will already touch the
54:       # upated_at columns up the reply chain to the story once
55:       comment.parent_comment&.update_column(:last_reply_at, Time.current)
56:       NotifyCommentJob.perform_later(comment)
57:       render_created_comment(comment)
58:     else
59:       comment.score = 1
60:       comment.current_vote = {vote: 1}
61: 
62:       preview comment
63:     end
64:   end
65: 
66:   def show
67:     if !(comment = find_comment)
68:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 404
69:     end
70:     if !comment.is_editable_by_user?(@user)
71:       return redirect_to Routes.comment_target_path(comment, true)
72:     end
73: 
74:     render partial: "comment",
75:       layout: false,
76:       content_type: "text/html",
77:       locals: {
78:         comment: comment,
79:         show_tree_lines: params[:show_tree_lines]
80:       }
81:   end
82: 
83:   def show_short_id
84:     if !(comment = find_comment)
85:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
86:     end
87: 
88:     render json: comment.as_json
89:   end
90: 
91:   def redirect_from_short_id
92:     if @user&.is_admin? && params[:id] =~ /\A\d+\z/
93:       redirect_to Routes.comment_target_path(Comment.find(params[:id]), true)
94:     end
95: 
96:     url = Rails.cache.fetch("c_#{params[:id]}", expires_in: nil) do
97:       story = Story.joins("join stories as s2 on stories.id = coalesce(s2.merged_story_id, s2.id) join comments on comments.story_id = s2.id")
98:         .where(comments: {short_id: params[:id]})
99:         .select(:short_id, :title)
100:         .first
101: 
102:       render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400 and return unless story
103:       Routes.title_path story, anchor: "c_#{params[:id]}"
104:     end
105: 
106:     # having looked up the given id as a comment short_id, we know it's safe to interpolate
107:     redirect_to url
108:   end
109: 
110:   def edit
111:     if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_editable_by_user?(@user))
112:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
113:     end
114: 
115:     if request.xhr?
116:       render partial: "commentbox", layout: false,
117:         content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment: comment}
118:     else
119:       render "_commentbox", locals: {comment: comment, parents: comment.parents, redirect_on_submit: true}
120:     end
121:   end
122: 
123:   def reply
124:     if !(parent_comment = find_comment)
125:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
126:     end
127: 
128:     story = parent_comment.story
129:     comment = story.comments.build
130:     comment.parent_comment = parent_comment
131:     comment.comment = params[:comment].to_s
132:     comment.user = @user
133: 
134:     if !parent_comment.depth_permits_reply?
135:       ModNote.tattle_on_max_depth_limit(@user, parent_comment)
136:       if request.xhr?
137:         render partial: "too_deep"
138:       else
139:         render "_too_deep"
140:       end
141:       return
142:     end
143: 
144:     if request.xhr?
145:       render partial: "commentbox", locals: {comment: comment, story: story}
146:     else
147:       parents = comment.parents.for_presentation
148:       parents = CommentVoteHydrator.new(parents, @user)
149:       render "_commentbox", locals: {
150:         comment: comment,
151:         story: story,
152:         parents: parents
153:       }
154:     end
155:   end
156: 
157:   def delete
158:     if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_deletable_by_user?(@user))
159:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
160:     end
161: 
162:     comment.delete_for_user(@user, params[:reason])
163: 
164:     render partial: "comment", layout: false,
165:       content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment: comment}
166:   end
167: 
168:   def undelete
169:     if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_undeletable_by_user?(@user))
170:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
171:     end
172: 
173:     comment.undelete_for_user(@user)
174: 
175:     render partial: "comment", layout: false,
176:       content_type: "text/html", locals: {comment: comment}
177:   end
178: 
179:   def disown
180:     if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_disownable_by_user?(@user))
181:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
182:     end
183: 
184:     InactiveUser.disown! comment
185: 
186:     if request.xhr?
187:       comment = find_comment
188:       show_story = ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(params[:show_story])
189:       show_tree_lines = ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(params[:show_tree_lines])
190: 
191:       render partial: "comment", locals: {comment: comment, show_story: show_story, show_tree_lines: show_tree_lines}
192:     else
193:       redirect_back_or_to(root_path)
194:     end
195:   end
196: 
197:   def update
198:     if !((comment = find_comment) && comment.is_editable_by_user?(@user))
199:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
200:     end
201: 
202:     comment.comment = params[:comment]
203:     comment.last_edited_at = Time.current
204:     comment.hat_id = nil
205:     comment.hat = @user.wearable_hats.find_by(short_id: params[:hat_id])
206: 
207:     if params[:preview].blank? && comment.save
208:       votes = Vote.comment_votes_by_user_for_comment_ids_hash(@user.id, [comment.id])
209:       current_user_reply_parents = @user&.ids_replied_to([comment.id]) || Hash.new { false }
210:       comment.current_vote = votes[comment.id]
211:       comment.vote_summary = Vote.comment_vote_summaries([comment.id])[comment.id]
212:       comment.current_reply = current_user_reply_parents.has_key? comment.id
213:       # not calling comment.touch because the :touch on the parent_comment association will already
214:       # touch the updated_at columns up the reply chain to the story once
215:       comment.parent_comment&.touch(:last_reply_at)
216: 
217:       if !request.xhr?
218:         return redirect_to Routes.comment_target_path(comment, true)
219:       end
220: 
221:       if params[:redirect_on_submit].present?
222:         head :ok, x_location: Routes.comment_target_path(comment, true)
223:       else
224:         render_created_comment(comment, params[:show_tree_lines])
225:       end
226:     else
227:       comment.current_vote = {vote: 1}
228: 
229:       preview comment
230:     end
231:   end
232: 
233:   def unvote
234:     if !(comment = find_comment) || comment.is_gone?
235:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
236:     end
237: 
238:     Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
239:       0, comment.story_id, comment.id, @user.id, nil
240:     )
241: 
242:     render plain: "ok"
243:   end
244: 
245:   def upvote
246:     if !(comment = find_comment) || comment.is_gone?
247:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
248:     end
249: 
250:     Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
251:       1, comment.story_id, comment.id, @user.id, nil
252:     )
253: 
254:     render plain: "ok"
255:   end
256: 
257:   def flag
258:     if !(comment = find_comment) || comment.is_gone?
259:       return render plain: "can't find comment", status: 400
260:     end
261: 
262:     if !Vote::COMMENT_REASONS[params[:reason]]
263:       return render plain: "invalid reason", status: 400
264:     end
265: 
266:     if !@user.can_flag?(comment)
267:       return render plain: "not permitted to flag", status: 400
268:     end
269: 
270:     Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
271:       -1, comment.story_id, comment.id, @user.id, params[:reason]
272:     )
273: 
274:     render plain: "ok"
275:   end

278:     @rss_link ||= {
279:       title: "RSS 2.0 - Newest Comments",
280:       href: user_token_link(comments_url(format: :rss))
281:     }
282: 
283:     @title = "Newest Comments"
284: 
285:     @page = params[:page].to_i
286:     if @page == 0
287:       @page = 1
288:     elsif @page < 0 || @page > (2**32)
289:       raise ActionController::RoutingError.new("page out of bounds")
290:     end
291: 
292:     @comments = Comment.accessible_to_user(@user)
293:       .not_on_story_hidden_by(@user)
294:       .filter_tags(filtered_tags.map(&:id))
295:       .order(id: :desc)
296:       .includes(:user, :hat, story: :user)
297:       .joins(:story).where.not(stories: {is_deleted: true})
298:       .limit(COMMENTS_PER_PAGE)
299:       .offset((@page - 1) * COMMENTS_PER_PAGE)
300: 
301:     @has_more = true # don't bother querying for the number of comments here
302: 
303:     @comments = CommentVoteHydrator.new(@comments, @user)
304: 
305:     @last_read_timestamp = if params[:last_read_timestamp]
306:       Time.zone.at(params[:last_read_timestamp].to_i)
307:     else
308:       @user&.last_read_newest_comment
309:     end
310: 
311:     @user&.touch(:last_read_newest_comment)
312:

314:     unless last_read_marker_shown

4:   before_action :find_story!
5:   before_action :show_title_h1
6: 
7:   def edit
8:     @title = "Edit Story"
9: 
10:     if @story.merged_into_story
11:       @story.merge_story_short_id = @story.merged_into_story.short_id
12:       User.update_counters @story.user_id, karma: (@story.votes.count * -2)
13:     end
14:   end
15: 
16:   def update
17:     @story.last_edited_at = Time.current
18:     @story.is_deleted = false
19:     @story.editor = @user
20:     update_story_attributes
21: 
22:     if @story.save
23:       redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
24:     else
25:       render action: "edit"
26:     end
27:   end
28: 
29:   def undelete
30:     @story.is_deleted = false
31:     @story.editor = @user
32:     update_story_attributes
33: 
34:     if @story.save
35:       Keystore.increment_value_for("user:#{@story.user.id}:stories_deleted", -1)
36:     end
37: 
38:     redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
39:   end
40: 
41:   def destroy
42:     update_story_attributes
43: 
44:     if @story.user_id != @user.id && @story.moderation_reason.blank?
45:       @story.errors.add(:moderation_reason, message: "is required")
46:       return render action: "edit"
47:     end
48: 
49:     @story.is_deleted = true
50:     @story.editor = @user
51: 
52:     if @story.save
53:       Keystore.increment_value_for("user:#{@story.user.id}:stories_deleted")
54:       Mastodon.delete_post(@story)
55:     end
56: 
57:     redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
58:   end

60:   private

62:   def story_params
63:     ps = params.require(:story).permit(
64:       :title, :url, :description, :moderation_reason,
65:       :merge_story_short_id, :is_unavailable, :user_is_author, :user_is_following,
66:       tags: []
67:     )
68:     ps[:tags] = Tag.where(tag: ps[:tags] || @story.tags.map(&:tag))
69:     ps
70:   end
71: 
72:   def update_story_attributes

21:         tag_error += if t.privileged?

8:   def stories_submitted_content(user, showing_user)
9:     tag = showing_user.most_common_story_tag
10: 
11:     stories_submitted = showing_user.stories_submitted_count
12:     stories_deleted = showing_user.stories_deleted_count
13:     stories_displayed = stories_submitted - stories_deleted
14: 
15:     capture do
16:       concat link_to(stories_displayed, newest_by_user_path(showing_user))
17: 
18:       concat(" (+#{stories_deleted} deleted)") if user&.is_moderator? && stories_deleted > 0
19:

22:         concat tag_link(tag)

29:

39:   def styled_user_link user, content = nil, parent = nil, html_options = {}
40:     html_options[:class] ||= []
41:     if content.is_a?(Story) && content.user_is_author?
42:       html_options[:class].push "user_is_author"
43:     end
44:     if content.is_a?(Comment) && content.story&.user_is_author? && content.story.user_id == user.id
45:       html_options[:class].push "user_is_author"
46:       html_options[:aria] = {label: "#{user.username} - Author"}
47:     end
48: 
49:     if content.is_a?(Comment) && content.story&.user_id == user.id
50:       html_options[:class].push "user_is_submitter"
51:       html_options[:aria] = {label: "#{user.username} - Submitter"}
52:     end
53: 
54:     if !user.is_active?
55:       html_options[:class].push "inactive_user"
56:       html_options[:aria] = {label: "#{user.username} - Inactive user"}
57:     end
58:     if user.is_new?
59:       html_options[:class].push "new_user"
60:       html_options[:aria] = {label: "#{user.username} - New user"}
61:     end
62:     html_options.delete(:class) if html_options[:class].empty?
63: 
64:     link_to(user.username, user_path(user), html_options)
65:   end

3: class EmailReplyMailer < ApplicationMailer
4:   def reply(comment, user)
5:     @comment = comment
6:     @user = user
7: 
8:     @replied_to = "you"
9:     if @comment.parent_comment.nil?
10:       @replied_to = "your story"
11:     elsif @comment.parent_comment.user != @user
12:       @replied_to = @comment.parent_comment.user.username
13:     end
14: 
15:     # threading
16:     set_headers
17: 
18:     mail(
19:       to: user.email,
20:       subject: "[#{Rails.application.name}] Reply from " \
21:                   "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}"
22:     )
23:   end
24: 
25:   def mention(comment, user)
26:     @comment = comment
27:     @user = user
28: 
29:     set_headers
30: 
31:     mail(
32:       to: user.email,
33:       subject: "[#{Rails.application.name}] Mention from " \
34:                   "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}"
35:     )
36:   end
37: 
38:   private
39: 
40:   def set_headers
41:     headers "Message-Id" => @comment.mailing_list_message_id,
42:       "References" => ([@comment.story.mailing_list_message_id] + @comment.parents.map(&:mailing_list_message_id))
43:         .map { |r| "<#{r}>" },
44:       "In-Reply-To" => @comment.parent_comment.present? ?
45:         @comment.parent_comment.mailing_list_message_id :
46:         @comment.story.mailing_list_message_id
47:   end
48: end

1: # typed: false
2: 
3: require "faker"
4: 
5: # This script creates real-ish fake data in your development database so it's easier to understand
6: # how a feature will work in real-world use. It has been written and expanded as needed, rather than
7: # try to perfectly simulate real-world data from scratch. If you need it for your project, please do
8: # improve it (no need for a separate PR).
9: 
10: # Disable launchy when populating with fake_data to prevent browser tabs from opening.
11: # As described in https://github.com/ryanb/letter_opener?tab=readme-ov-file#remote-alternatives
12: ENV["LAUNCHY_DRY_RUN"] = "true"
13: ENV["BROWSER"] = File::NULL
14: 
15: class FakeDataGenerator
16:   def initialize(users_count = 50, stories_count = 100, categories_count = 5)
17:     @users_count = users_count
18:     @stories_count = stories_count
19:     @categories_count = categories_count
20:   end
21: 
22:   # https://gist.github.com/searls/2859ad7e8941872edb9561eb965b7c76
23:   def lorem_paragraphs(paragraphs = (1..4), sentences = (1..10), words = (3..20))
24:     rand(paragraphs).times.map {
25:       rand(sentences).times.map {
26:         Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: rand(words))
27:       }.join(" ")
28:     }.join("\n\n")
29:   end
30: 
31:   def markdown_paragraphs
32:     lorem_paragraphs.split("\n\n").map { |sentence|
33:       sentence.split(" ").map { |word|
34:         if rand(100) < 1
35:           "_#{word}_"
36:         elsif rand(100) < 2
37:           "[#{word}](http://example.com/#{word})"
38:         else
39:           word
40:         end
41:       }.join(" ")
42:     }.join("\n\n")
43:   end
44: 
45:   def generate
46:     # not create! because fake_data can be run multiple times
47:     User.create username: "inactive-user", password: "inactive", email: "inactive-user@#{Rails.application.domain}"
48: 
49:     print "Users "
50:     users = []
51:     0.upto(@users_count).each do |i|
52:       print "."
53:       mod = User.moderators.sample
54:       name = Faker::Name.name
55:       password = "fake"
56:       create_args = {
57:         email: Faker::Internet.email(name: name),
58:         password: password,
59:         password_confirmation: password,
60:         username: Faker::Internet.user_name(specifier: name, separators: %w[_])[..23],
61:         created_at: (User::NEW_USER_DAYS + 1).days.ago,
62:         karma: Random.rand(User::MIN_KARMA_TO_FLAG * 2),
63:         about: Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 7),
64:         homepage: Faker::Internet.url,
65:         invited_by_user: User.select(&:can_invite?).sample
66:       }
67:       create_args[:is_admin] = true if i % 8 == 0
68:       begin
69:         users << User.create!(create_args)
70:         if i % 7 == 0
71:           users.last.grant_moderatorship_by_user!(mod)
72:         end
73:         if i % 6 == 0
74:           users.last.disable_invite_by_user_for_reason!(mod, Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 5))
75:         end
76:       rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
77:         puts "caught #{e}"
78:         next if e.message == "Validation failed: Username has already been taken"
79:       end
80:     end
81:     users.compact!
82:     puts
83: 
84:     print "Categories "
85:     categories = []
86:     @categories_count.times do
87:       print "."
88:       cat = Faker::Lorem.word.capitalize
89:       categories << Category.create!(category: cat) unless Category.find_by(category: cat)
90:     end
91:     puts
92: 
93:     print "Stories "
94:     stories = []
95:     @stories_count.times do |i|
96:       print "."
97:       user = users[Random.rand(@users_count - 1)]
98:       title = Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 3)
99:       category = categories.sample
100:       tag_name = title.split(" ").first.downcase
101:       tag = Tag.find_by tag: tag_name
102:       tag ||= Tag.create!(
103:         tag: tag_name,
104:         category: category,
105:         description: Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: Random.rand(2..15))[...100]
106:       )
107:       url = Faker::Internet.url
108:       description = nil
109:       if i % 10 == 0
110:         description = markdown_paragraphs
111:         url = nil unless i % 7 == 0
112:       end
113:       create_args = {
114:         user: user,
115:         title: title,
116:         url: url,
117:         description: description,
118:         tags: [tag]
119:       }
120:       story = Story.create!(create_args)
121:       StoryText.create!({
122:         id: story.id,
123:         title: story.title,
124:         description: story.description,
125:         body: markdown_paragraphs
126:       })
127:       stories << story
128:     end
129:     puts
130: 
131:     # The stories are created here and deleted after adding comments and other interactions.
132:     deleted_stories = []
133:     (@stories_count / 10).times do |i|
134:       user = users[Random.rand(@users_count - 1)]
135:       title = Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 3)
136:       category = categories[Random.rand(@categories_count - 1)]
137:       tag_name = title.split(" ").first.downcase
138:       tag = Tag.find_by tag: tag_name
139:       tag ||= Tag.create! tag: tag_name, category: category
140:       url = Faker::Internet.url
141:       create_args = {
142:         user: user,
143:         title: title,
144:         url: url,
145:         description: markdown_paragraphs,
146:         tags: [tag],
147:         is_deleted: true,
148:         editor: user
149:       }
150:       stories << Story.create!(create_args)
151:       deleted_stories << stories.last if i % 30 == 0
152:     end
153: 
154:     print "SavedStories "
155:     (@stories_count / 10).times do
156:       print "."
157:       user = users[Random.rand(@users_count - 1)]
158:       story = stories[Random.rand(@stories_count - 1)]
159:       SavedStory.save_story_for_user(story.id, user.id)
160:     end
161:     puts
162: 
163:     print "Comments "
164:     comments = []
165:     replies = {}
166:     stories.each do |x|
167:       print "."
168:       next unless x.accepting_comments?
169:       Random.rand(1..15).times do |i|
170:         create_args = {
171:           user: users[Random.rand(@users_count - 1)],
172:           comment: markdown_paragraphs,
173:           story_id: x.id
174:         }
175:         comments << Comment.create!(create_args)
176: 
177:         # Replies to comments
178:         if i.odd?
179:           parent_comment_id = comments.last.id
180:           create_args = {
181:             user: x.user,
182:             comment: markdown_paragraphs,
183:             story_id: x.id,
184:             parent_comment_id: parent_comment_id
185:           }
186:           comments << Comment.create!(create_args)
187:           replies[[x.user.id, parent_comment_id]] = true
188:         end
189:       end
190:     end
191:     puts
192: 
193:     print "Comment Flags and Votes "
194:     comments.each do |c|
195:       print "."
196:       upvotes = Random.rand(20)
197:       # Take one extra user to have a chance to flag the comment
198:       voting_users = users.sample(upvotes + 1)
199:       if Random.rand(100) > 95
200:         u = voting_users[0]
201:         # can't flag if you've already replied, just skip
202:         next if replies.has_key? [u.id, c.id]
203:         Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
204:           -1,
205:           c.story_id,
206:           c.id,
207:           u.id,
208:           Vote::COMMENT_REASONS.keys[Random.rand(Vote::COMMENT_REASONS.keys.length)]
209:         )
210:       end
211: 
212:       voting_users.slice(1..).each do |u|
213:         Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
214:           1,
215:           c.story_id,
216:           c.id,
217:           u.id,
218:           nil
219:         )
220:       end
221:     end
222:     puts
223: 
224:     print "Story Flags and Votes "
225:     stories.each do |s|
226:       print "."
227:       upvotes = Random.rand(20)
228:       # Take one extra user to have a chance to flag the story
229:       voting_users = users.sample(upvotes + 1)
230:       if Random.rand(100) > 95
231:         u = voting_users[0]
232:         Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
233:           -1,
234:           s.id,
235:           nil,
236:           u.id,
237:           Vote::STORY_REASONS.keys[Random.rand(Vote::STORY_REASONS.keys.length)]
238:         )
239:       end
240: 
241:       voting_users.slice(1..).each do |u|
242:         Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
243:           1,
244:           s.id,
245:           nil,
246:           u.id,
247:           nil
248:         )
249:       end
250:     end
251:     puts
252: 
253:     print "Hats "
254:     hats = []
255:     (@users_count / 2).times do |i|
256:       print "."
257:       suffixes = ["Developer", "Founder", "User", "Contributor", "Creator"]
258:       hat_wearer = users[i + 1]
259:       create_args = {
260:         user: hat_wearer,
261:         granted_by_user: users[0],
262:         hat: Faker::Lorem.word.capitalize + " " + suffixes[Random.rand(5)],
263:         link: Faker::Internet.url
264:       }
265:       hat = Hat.create!(create_args)
266:       if i.odd?
267:         hat.doff_by_user_with_reason(hat_wearer, Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 5))
268:       end
269:       hats << hat
270:     end
271:     puts
272: 
273:     ### Moderation ###
274: 
275:     print "Comments (delete/undelete) "
276:     comments.each_with_index do |comment, i|
277:       print "."
278:       comment_mod = User.moderators.sample
279:       if i % 7 == 0
280:         comment.delete_for_user(comment_mod, Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 8))
281:         comment.undelete_for_user(comment_mod) if i.even?
282:       end
283:     end
284:     puts
285: 
286:     print "Delete stories by submitter/mods "
287:     deleted_stories.each do |story|
288:       print "."
289:       if story.id.even?
290:         story.editor = story.user
291:       else
292:         story.editor = User.moderators.sample
293:         story.moderation_reason = Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 5)
294:       end
295:       story.tags_was = story.tags.to_a
296:       story.update!(is_deleted: true)
297:     end
298:     puts
299: 
300:     print "Doff hats "
301:     2.times do
302:       print "."
303:       hat = hats[Random.rand(hats.length - 1)]
304:       hat.doff_by_user_with_reason(User.moderators.sample,
305:         Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 5))
306:     end
307:     puts
308: 
309:     print "Ban Users " # don't delete inactive-user or test
310:     User.where("id > 2").sample(2).each_with_index do |user, i|
311:       print "."
312:       user.ban_by_user_for_reason!(User.moderators.sample,
313:         Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 5))
314:       if i.even?
315:         user.unban_by_user!(User.moderators.sample, Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 5))
316:       end
317:     end
318:     puts
319: 
320:     print "Merge Stories "
321:     5.times do
322:       print "."
323:       story = stories[Random.rand(stories.length - 1)]
324:       second_story = stories[Random.rand(stories.length - 1)]
325:       while second_story == story
326:         second_story = stories[Random.rand(stories.length - 1)]
327:       end
328:       story.merged_story_id = second_story.id
329:       story.editing_from_suggestions = true
330:       story.moderation_reason = Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 5)
331:       story.tags_was = story.tags.to_a
332:       story.save!
333:     end
334:     puts
335: 
336:     print "Editing Stories "
337:     5.times do
338:       print "."
339:       story = stories[Random.rand(stories.length - 1)]
340:       story.title = Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 4)
341:       story.editing_from_suggestions = true
342:       story.moderation_reason = Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 5)
343:       story.tags_was = story.tags.to_a
344:       story.save!
345:     end
346:     puts
347: 
348:     print "Deleting stories "
349:     5.times do
350:       print "."
351:       story = stories[Random.rand(stories.length - 1)]
352:       story.is_deleted = true
353:       story.editing_from_suggestions = true
354:       story.moderation_reason = Faker::Lorem.sentence(word_count: 5)
355:       story.tags_was = story.tags.to_a
356:       story.save!
357:     end
358:     puts

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env ruby
2: 
3: ENV["RAILS_ENV"] ||= "production"
4: 
5: require File.expand_path("../../config/environment", __FILE__)
6: 
7: exit unless Mastodon.enabled? && Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.bot_name
8: 
9: Story.to_mastodon.each_with_index do |s, i|
10:   if i > 0
11:     sleep 2
12:   end
13: 
14:   tags = s.tags.pluck(:tag).map { |t| " #" + t }.join("")
15: 
16:   via = if s.user.mastodon_username.present?
17:     (s.user_is_author? ? " by" : " via") + " @#{s.user.mastodon_username}@#{s.user.mastodon_instance}"
18:   else
19:     " "
20:   end
21: 
22:   link_status = via + " " +
23:     ("X" * Mastodon::LINK_LENGTH) + tags +
24:     (s.url.present? ? "\n" + ("X" * Mastodon::LINK_LENGTH) : "")
25: 
26:   status = via + " " + Routes.story_short_id_url(s) + tags +
27:     (s.url.present? ? "\n" + s.url : "")
28: 
29:   left_len = Mastodon::MAX_STATUS_LENGTH - link_status.length
30: 
31:   title = s.title
32: 
33:   # if there are any urls in the title, they should be sized (at least) to a url
34:   left_len -= title.scan(/[^ ]\.[a-z]{2,10}/i)
35:     .map { |_u| [0, Mastodon::LINK_LENGTH].max }
36:     .inject(:+)
37:     .to_i
38: 
39:   if left_len < -3
40:     left_len = -3
41:   end
42: 
43:   status = if title.bytesize > left_len
44:     title[0, left_len - 3] + "..." + status
45:   else
46:     title + status
47:   end
48: 
49:   begin
50:     res = Mastodon.post(status)
51:     post = JSON.parse(res.body)
52:     s.update_column(:mastodon_id, post["id"])
53:   rescue => e
54:     s.update_column(:mastodon_id, 0)
55:     puts "failed posting story #{s.id} (#{status.inspect}): #{e.inspect}\n#{res.inspect}"
56:     exit
57:   end
58: end

(End of file - total 58 lines)

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env ruby
2: 
3: require File.expand_path("../../config/environment", __FILE__)
4: 
5: class String
6:   def quoted_printable(encoded_word = false)
7:     string = [self].pack("M")
8: 
9:     return string unless encoded_word
10: 
11:     q_encode_word = ->(w) { "=?UTF-8?Q?#{w}?=" }
12: 
13:     string
14:       # Undo linebreaks from #pack("M") because we'll be adding characters
15:       .gsub("=\n", "")
16:       # Question marks are delimiters in q-encoding so must be escaped
17:       .gsub("?", "=3F")
18:       # Spaces are insignificant in q-encoding so must be escaped
19:       .gsub(/\s+/, " _")
20:       # Take each space-separated word, then q-encode
21:       .split(" ").map(&q_encode_word)
22:       # Recombine words then word wrap at 75 characters
23:       .join(" ").word_wrap(75)
24:       # Compose final string, folding headers per rfc 2822 section 2.2.3
25:       .lines.join("\t")
26:   end
27: 
28:   # like ActionView::Helpers::TextHelper but preserve > and indentation when
29:   # wrapping lines
30:   def word_wrap(len)
31:     split("\n").collect do |line|
32:       if line.length <= len
33:         line
34:       elsif (m = line.match(/^(> ?|   +)(.*)/))
35:         ind = m[1]
36:         if len - ind.length <= 0
37:           ind = "    "
38:         end
39:         m[2].gsub(/(.{1,#{len - ind.length}})(\s+|$)/, "#{ind}\\1\n").strip
40:       else
41:         line.gsub(/(.{1,#{len}})(\s+|$)/, "\\1\n").strip
42:       end
43:     end * "\n"
44:   end
45: end
46: 
47: def story_subject(story, prefix = "")
48:   ss = "#{prefix}#{story.title}"
49: 
50:   story.tags.sort_by(&:tag).each do |t|
51:     ss << " [#{t.tag}]"
52:   end
53: 
54:   ss.quoted_printable(true)
55: end
56: 
57: if __FILE__ == $PROGRAM_NAME
58:   EMAIL_WIDTH = 72
59:   LAST_STORY_KEY = "mailing:last_story_id".freeze
60:   LAST_COMMENT_KEY = "mailing:last_comment_id".freeze
61: 
62:   mailing_list_users = User.where("mailing_list_mode > 0").select(&:is_active?)
63: 
64:   last_story_id = (Keystore.value_for(LAST_STORY_KEY) || Story.last && Story.last.id).to_i
65: 
66:   # Paranoia: only search back three days so that if last_story_id is oddly low we don't start
67:   # sending every story from the beginning of time, or if mailing list mode breaks for more than a
68:   # few days we won't bury them in email.
69:   Story
70:     .where("id > ? AND is_deleted = ? AND created_at >= ?", last_story_id, false, 3.days.ago)
71:     .order(:id)
72:     .each do |s|
73:     StoryText.fill_cache!(s)
74: 
75:     mailing_list_users.each do |u|
76:       if (s.tags.map(&:id) & u.tag_filters.map(&:tag_id)).any?
77:         # story has tags this user has filtered out
78:         next
79:       end
80: 
81:       if s.is_hidden_by_user?(u)
82:         # user has hidden this story
83:         next
84:       end
85: 
86:       list = "#{Rails.application.shortname}-#{u.mailing_list_token}@#{Rails.application.domain}"
87: 
88:       IO.popen(
89:         [{}, "/usr/sbin/sendmail", "-i", "-f", "nobody@#{Rails.application.domain}", u.email],
90:         "w"
91:       ) do |mail|
92:         mail.puts "From: #{s.user.username} <#{s.user.username}@#{Rails.application.domain}>"
93:         mail.puts "X-Is-Author: #{s.user_is_author?}"
94:         mail.puts "Reply-To: #{list}"
95:         mail.puts "To: #{list}"
96:         mail.puts "X-BeenThere: #{list}"
97:         mail.puts "List-Id: #{Rails.application.name} <#{list}>"
98:         mail.puts "List-Unsubscribe: <#{Rails.application.root_url}settings>"
99:         mail.puts "Precedence: list"
100:         mail.puts "MIME-Version: 1.0"
101:         mail.puts "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"utf-8\""
102:         mail.puts "Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable"
103:         mail.puts "Message-ID: <#{s.mailing_list_message_id}>"
104:         mail.puts "Date: " << s.created_at.strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S %z")
105:         mail.puts "Subject: " << story_subject(s)
106:         mail.puts ""
107: 
108:         body = []
109: 
110:         if s.description.present?
111:           body.push s.description.to_s.word_wrap(EMAIL_WIDTH)
112:           body.push ""
113:         end
114: 
115:         body.push "Vote: #{Routes.story_short_id_url s}"
116: 
117:         if s.url.present?
118:           body.push ""
119:           body.push "Via: #{s.url}"
120: 
121:           StoryText.cached?(s) do |text|
122:             body.push ""
123:             body.push text.to_s.word_wrap(EMAIL_WIDTH)
124:           end
125:         end
126: 
127:         mail.puts body.join("\n").quoted_printable
128:       end
129:     end
130: 
131:     last_story_id = s.id
132:   end
133: 
134:   Keystore.put(LAST_STORY_KEY, last_story_id)
135: 
136:   # repeat for comments
137: 
138:   last_comment_id = (Keystore.value_for(LAST_COMMENT_KEY) || Comment.last && Comment.last.id).to_i
139: 
140:   # Paranoia: only search back three days so that if last_comment_id is oddly low we don't start
141:   # sending every comment from the beginning of time, or if mailing list mode breaks for more than a
142:   # few days we won't bury them in email.
143:   Comment.where(
144:     "id > ? AND (is_deleted = ? AND is_moderated = ?) AND created_at >= ?",
145:     last_comment_id,
146:     false,
147:     false,
148:     3.days.ago
149:   ).order(:id).each do |c|
150:     # allow some time for newer comments to be edited before sending them out
151:     if (Time.current - c.last_edited_at) < 2.minutes
152:       break
153:     end
154: 
155:     mailing_list_users.each do |u|
156:       if u.mailing_list_mode == 2
157:         # stories only
158:         next
159:       end
160: 
161:       if (c.story.tags.map(&:id) & u.tag_filters.map(&:tag_id)).any?
162:         # story has tags this user has filtered out
163:         next
164:       end
165: 
166:       if c.story.is_hidden_by_user?(u)
167:         # user has hidden this story
168:         next
169:       end
170: 
171:       domain = Rails.application.domain
172:       list = "#{Rails.application.shortname}-#{u.mailing_list_token}@#{Rails.application.domain}"
173: 
174:       IO.popen([{}, "/usr/sbin/sendmail", "-i", "-f", "nobody@#{domain}", u.email], "w") do |mail|
175:         from = "From: \"#{c.user.username}"
176:         if c.hat
177:           from << " (#{c.hat.hat})"
178:         end
179:         from << "\" <#{c.user.username}@#{domain}>"
180:         mail.puts from
181: 
182:         mail.puts "Reply-To: #{list}"
183:         mail.puts "To: #{list}"
184:         mail.puts "List-Id: #{Rails.application.name} <#{list}>"
185:         mail.puts "List-Unsubscribe: <#{Rails.application.root_url}settings>"
186:         mail.puts "Precedence: list"
187:         mail.puts "MIME-Version: 1.0"
188:         mail.puts "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"utf-8\""
189:         mail.puts "Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable"
190:         mail.puts "Message-ID: <#{c.mailing_list_message_id}>"
191: 
192:         if c.parent_comment_id
193:           mail.puts "In-Reply-To: <#{c.parent_comment.mailing_list_message_id}>"
194:         else
195:           mail.puts "In-Reply-To: <#{c.story.mailing_list_message_id}>"
196:         end
197: 
198:         refs = ([c.story.mailing_list_message_id] +
199:           c.parents.map(&:mailing_list_message_id))
200:           .map { |r| "<#{r}>" }
201:         mail.puts "References: #{refs.join(" ")}"
202: 
203:         mail.puts "Date: " << c.created_at.strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S %z")
204:         mail.puts "Subject: " << story_subject(c.story, "Re: ")
205:         mail.puts ""
206: 
207:         body = []
208: 
209:         # if the comment has hard line breaks at <80, it likely came from an
210:         # email, so don't re-wrap it at something shorter
211:         com = c.comment.to_s
212:         com_lines = com.split("\n")
213:         if com_lines.length > 1 && com_lines.first.length < 80
214:           body.push com.word_wrap(80)
215:         else
216:           body.push com.word_wrap(EMAIL_WIDTH)
217:         end
218: 
219:         body.push ""
220:         body.push "-- "
221:         body.push "Vote: #{Routes.comment_short_id_url c}"
222: 
223:         mail.puts body.join("\n").quoted_printable
224:       end
225:     end
226: 
227:     last_comment_id = c.id
228:   end
229: 
230:   Keystore.put(LAST_COMMENT_KEY, last_comment_id)
231: end

1: class ModActivity < ApplicationRecord
2:   belongs_to :item, polymorphic: true
3: 
4:   validates :item_id, uniqueness: {scope: [:item_type]}
5:   validates :item_type, presence: true, length: {maximum: 255}
6: 
7:   scope :with_item, -> {
8:     joins("left outer join moderations on (item_type = 'Moderation' and item_id = moderations.id)")
9:       .joins("left outer join mod_notes on (item_type = 'ModNote' and item_id = mod_notes.id)")
10:       .joins("left outer join mod_mails on (item_type = 'ModMail' and item_id = mod_mails.id)")
11:       .includes(:item)
12:   }
13:   # the moderation is about this user, or a thing they submitted
14:   # using this output generates a lot of 1 + n but #539 will remove that problem
15:   scope :user, ->(user) {
16:     with_item
17:       .joins("left outer join stories on stories.id = moderations.story_id")
18:       .joins("left outer join comments on comments.id = moderations.comment_id")
19:       .joins("left outer join mod_mail_recipients on mod_mails.id = mod_mail_recipients.mod_mail_id")
20:       # string here because rails won't allow .where() to extend across polymorphic join
21:       .where("
22:         (
23:           mod_notes.id is not null and
24:           mod_notes.user_id = :user_id
25:         ) or
26:         (
27:           moderations.id is not null and
28:           (
29:             moderations.user_id = :user_id or
30:             stories.user_id = :user_id or
31:             comments.user_id = :user_id
32:           )
33:         ) or
34:         (
35:           mod_mails.id is not null and
36:           mod_mail_recipients.user_id = :user_id
37:         )
38:         ", {user_id: user.id})
39:   }
40: 
41:   include Token
42: 
43:   def self.create_for!(item)
44:     updated_at = item.respond_to?(:updated_at) ? item.updated_at : item.created_at
45:     create! item: item, created_at: item.created_at, updated_at:
46:   end
47: end

3: class Message < ApplicationRecord
4:   belongs_to :recipient,
5:     class_name: "User",
6:     foreign_key: "recipient_user_id",
7:     inverse_of: :received_messages
8:   belongs_to :author,
9:     class_name: "User",
10:     foreign_key: "author_user_id",
11:     inverse_of: :sent_messages,
12:     optional: true
13:   belongs_to :hat,
14:     optional: true
15:   has_one :notification,
16:     as: :notifiable,
17:     dependent: :restrict_with_exception
18: 
19:   include Token
20: 
21:   attribute :mod_note, :boolean
22:   attr_reader :recipient_username
23: 
24:   validates :subject, length: {in: 1..100}
25:   validates :body, length: {maximum: 70_000}, on: :update # for weird old data
26:   validates :body, length: {within: 5..8_192}, on: :create # max from 2024-10-28 on, min changed 2026-01-12
27:   validates :short_id, presence: true, uniqueness: {case_sensitive: false}, length: {maximum: 30}
28:   validates :deleted_by_author, :deleted_by_recipient, inclusion: {in: [true, false]}
29:   validate :hat do
30:     next if hat.blank?
31:     if author.blank? || author.wearable_hats.exclude?(hat)
32:       errors.add(:hat, "not wearable by author")
33:     end
34:   end
35: 
36:   scope :inbox, ->(user) {
37:     where(
38:       recipient: user,
39:       deleted_by_recipient: false
40:     ).preload(:author, :hat, :notification, :recipient).order(id: :asc)
41:   }
42:   scope :outbox, ->(user) {
43:     where(
44:       author: user,
45:       deleted_by_author: false
46:     ).preload(:author, :hat, :notification, :recipient).order(id: :asc)
47:   }
48: 
49:   before_validation :assign_short_id, on: :create
50:   after_save :check_for_both_deleted
51: 
52:   def as_json(_options = {})
53:     attrs = [
54:       :short_id,
55:       :created_at,
56:       :subject,
57:       :body,
58:       :deleted_by_author,
59:       :deleted_by_recipient
60:     ]
61: 
62:     h = super(only: attrs)
63: 
64:     h[:author_username] = author.try(:username)
65:     h[:recipient_username] = recipient.try(:username)
66: 
67:     h
68:   end
69: 
70:   def assign_short_id
71:     self.short_id = ShortId.new(self.class).generate
72:   end
73: 
74:   def author_username
75:     if author
76:       author.username
77:     else
78:       "System"
79:     end
80:   end
81: 
82:   def check_for_both_deleted
83:     if deleted_by_author? && deleted_by_recipient?
84:       destroy!
85:     end
86:   end
87: 
88:   def recipient_username=(username)
89:     self.recipient_user_id = nil
90: 
91:     if (u = User.find_by(username: username))
92:       self.recipient_user_id = u.id
93:       @recipient_username = username
94:     else
95:       errors.add(:recipient_username, "is not a valid user")
96:     end
97:   end
98: 
99:   def linkified_body
100:     Markdowner.to_html(body, as_of: created_at)
101:   end
102: 
103:   def plaintext_body
104:     # TODO: linkify then strip tags and convert entities back
105:     body.to_s
106:   end
107: 
108:   def to_param
109:     short_id
110:   end
111: end

3: class StoriesPaginator
4:   attr_accessor :per_page
5: 
6:   STORIES_PER_PAGE = 25
7: 
8:   def initialize(scope, page = 1, user = nil)
9:     @scope = scope
10:     @page = page
11:     @user = user
12:     @per_page = STORIES_PER_PAGE
13:   end
14: 
15:   def get
16:     with_pagination_info @scope.limit(per_page + 1)
17:       .offset((@page - 1) * per_page)
18:       .for_presentation
19:   end
20: 
21:   private
22: 
23:   def with_pagination_info(scope)
24:     scope = scope.to_a
25:     show_more = scope.count > per_page
26:     scope.pop if show_more
27: 
28:     [cache_votes(scope), show_more]
29:   end
30: 
31:   def cache_votes(scope)
32:     if @user
33:       votes = Vote.votes_by_user_for_stories_hash(@user.id, scope.map(&:id))
34: 
35:       hs = HiddenStory.where(user_id: @user.id, story_id: scope.map(&:id)).map(&:story_id)
36:       ss = SavedStory.where(user_id: @user.id, story_id: scope.map(&:id)).map(&:story_id)
37: 
38:       scope.each do |s|
39:         s.current_vote = votes[s.id]
40:         if hs.include?(s.id)
41:           s.is_hidden_by_cur_user = true
42:         end
43:         if ss.include?(s.id)
44:           s.is_saved_by_cur_user = true
45:         end
46:       end
47:     end
48:     scope
49:   end
50: end

3: class Tag < ApplicationRecord
4:   belongs_to :category
5:   has_many :taggings, dependent: :delete_all
6:   has_many :stories, through: :taggings
7:   has_many :tag_filters, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
8:   has_many :filtering_users,
9:     class_name: "User",
10:     through: :tag_filters,
11:     source: :user,
12:     dependent: :delete_all
13:   has_one :moderation, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
14:   has_many :suggested_taggings, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
15: 
16:   after_save :log_modifications
17: 
18:   include Token
19: 
20:   attr_accessor :edit_user_id, :stories_count
21:   attr_writer :filtered_count
22: 
23:   validates :tag, length: {maximum: 25}, presence: true,
24:     uniqueness: {case_sensitive: true},
25:     format: {with: /\A[A-Za-z0-9_\-+]+\z/}
26:   validates :description, length: {maximum: 100}
27:   validates :hotness_mod, inclusion: {in: -10..10}
28:   validates :permit_by_new_users, :privileged, :active, :is_media,
29:     inclusion: {in: [true, false]}
30: 
31:   scope :active, -> { where(active: true) }
32:   scope :not_permitted_for_new_users, -> { where(permit_by_new_users: false) }
33:   scope :related, ->(tag) {
34:     active
35:       .joins(:taggings)
36:       .where(taggings: {story_id: Tagging.where(tag: tag).select(:story_id)})
37:       .where.not(id: [tag, 67]) # 67 = programming, the catch-all
38:       .where.not(is_media: true)
39:       .group(:id)
40:       .order(Arel.sql("COUNT(*) desc"))
41:       .limit(8)
42:   }
43: 
44:   def to_param
45:     tag
46:   end
47: 
48:   def self.all_with_filtered_counts_for(user)
49:     counts = TagFilter.group(:tag_id).count
50: 
51:     Tag.active.order(:tag).select { |t| t.can_be_applied_by?(user) }.map { |t|
52:       t.filtered_count = counts[t.id].to_i
53:       t
54:     }
55:   end
56: 
57:   def category_name
58:     category&.category
59:   end
60: 
61:   def category_name=(category)
62:     self.category = Category.find_by category: category
63:   end
64: 
65:   def css_class
66:     "tag tag_#{tag}" << (is_media? ? " tag_is_media" : "")
67:   end
68: 
69:   def user_can_filter?(user)
70:     active? && (!privileged? || user.try(:is_moderator?))
71:   end
72: 
73:   def can_be_applied_by?(user)
74:     if privileged?
75:       !!user.try(:is_moderator?)
76:     # do include tags they can't use so they submit and get error
77:     else
78:       true
79:     end
80:   end
81: 
82:   def filtered_count
83:     @filtered_count ||= TagFilter.where(tag_id: id).count
84:   end
85: 
86:   def log_modifications
87:     Moderation.create do |m|
88:       m.action = if id_previously_changed?
89:         "Created new tag " + attributes.map { |f, c| "with #{f} '#{c}'" }.join(", ")
90:       else
91:         "Updating tag #{tag}, " + saved_changes
92:           .map { |f, c| "changed #{f} from '#{c[0]}' to '#{c[1]}'" }.join(", ")
93:       end

1: # typed: false
2: 
3: # Finds the consistent most-heavily-flagged commenters. Requires flags to be spread over
4: # several comments and stories because anyone can have a bad thread or a bad day.
5: 
6: class FlaggedCommenters
7:   include IntervalHelper
8: 
9:   attr_reader :interval, :period, :cache_time
10: 
11:   def initialize(interval, cache_time = 30.minutes)
12:     @interval = interval
13:     @cache_time = cache_time
14:     length = time_interval(interval)
15:     @period = length[:dur].send(length[:intv].downcase).ago
16:   end
17: 
18:   def check_list_for(showing_user)
19:     commenters[showing_user.id]
20:   end
21: 
22:   # aggregates for all commenters; not just those receiving flags
23:   def aggregates
24:     Rails.cache.fetch("aggregates_#{interval}_#{cache_time}", expires_in: cache_time) {
25:       ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query("
26:         select
27:           stddev(sum_flags) as stddev,
28:           sum(sum_flags) as sum,
29:           avg(sum_flags) as avg,
30:           avg(n_comments) as n_comments,
31:           count(*) as n_commenters
32:         from (
33:           select
34:             sum(flags) as sum_flags,
35:             count(*) as n_comments
36:           from comments join users on comments.user_id = users.id
37:           where
38:             (comments.created_at >= '#{period}') and
39:             users.banned_at is null and
40:             users.deleted_at is null
41:           GROUP BY comments.user_id
42:         ) sums;
43:       ").first.symbolize_keys!
44:     }
45:   end
46: 
47:   def stddev_sum_flags
48:     aggregates[:stddev].to_f
49:   end
50: 
51:   def avg_sum_flags
52:     aggregates[:avg].to_f
53:   end
54: 
55:   def commenters
56:     Rails.cache.fetch("flagged_commenters_#{interval}_#{cache_time}",
57:       expires_in: cache_time) {
58:       rank = 0
59:       User.active.joins(:comments)
60:         .where("comments.created_at >= ?", period)
61:         .group("comments.user_id")
62:         .select("
63:           users.id, users.username,
64:           (sum(flags) - #{avg_sum_flags})/#{stddev_sum_flags} as sigma,
65:           count(distinct if(flags > 0, comments.id, null)) as n_comments,
66:           count(distinct if(flags > 0, story_id, null)) as n_stories,
67:           sum(flags) as n_flags,
68:           sum(flags)/count(distinct comments.id) as average_flags,
69:           (
70:             count(distinct if(flags > 0, comments.id, null)) /
71:             count(distinct comments.id)
72:           ) * 100 as percent_flagged")
73:         .having("n_comments > 4 and n_stories > 1 and n_flags >= 10 and percent_flagged > 10")
74:         .order(sigma: :desc)
75:         .limit(30)
76:         .each_with_object({}) { |u, hash|
77:           hash[u.id] = {
78:             username: u.username,
79:             rank: rank += 1,
80:             sigma: u.sigma,
81:             n_comments: u.n_comments,
82:             n_stories: u.n_stories,
83:             n_flags: u.n_flags,
84:             average_flags: u.average_flags,
85:             stddev: 0,
86:             percent_flagged: u.percent_flagged
87:           }
88:         }
89:     }
90:   end
91: end

3: class SearchController < ApplicationController
4:   before_action :show_title_h1
5:   before_action :ignore_searx
6: 
7:   def index
8:     @title = "Search"
9: 
10:     @search = Search.new(search_params, @user)
11: 
12:     if !@user && params[:q].to_s.starts_with?("https://")
13:       flash[:error] = "Sorry, you have to log in to search for a URL. We're getting hammered by a spambot with many thousands of IPs. More info at https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/issues/1814"
14:       @results = []
15:     else
16:       @results = @search.results
17:     end
18: 
19:     if @user && @search.results
20:       if params[:what] == "stories"
21:         votes = Vote.story_votes_by_user_for_story_ids_hash(@user.id, @search.results.map(&:id))
22:         @search.results.each do |r|
23:           r.current_vote = votes.try(:[], r.id)
24:         end
25:       end
26:       @results = if params[:what] == "comments"
27:         CommentVoteHydrator.new(@search.results, @user)
28:       else
29:         @search.results
30:       end
31:     end
32:   end
33: 
34:   private
35: 
36:   # searx is a meta-search engine, instances send endless garbage traffic to our most-expensive
37:   # endpoint https://github.com/searx/searx/blob/master/searx/settings.yml#L807
38:   # If you are maintaining a searx fork, please don't 'fix' your targeting of this site.
39:   def ignore_searx
40:     return unless params[:utf8] == "✓"
41:     @search = Search.new({results_count: 0}, nil)
42:     @results = []
43:     render :index
44:   end
45: 
46:   def search_params
47:     params.permit(:q, :what, :order, :page, :authenticity_token)
48:   end
49: end

55:   it "removes redundant http port 80 and https port 443" do
56:     expect(Story.new(url: "http://example.com:80").url).to eq("http://example.com")
57:     expect(Story.new(url: "http://example.com:80/").url).to eq("http://example.com/")
58:     expect(Story.new(url: "https://example.com:443").url).to eq("https://example.com")
59:     expect(Story.new(url: "https://example.com:443/").url).to eq("https://example.com/")

64:     expect(Story.new(url: "http://a.com?utm_term=track&c=d").url).to eq("http://a.com?c=d")
65:     expect(Story.new(url: "http://a.com?a=b&utm_term=track&c=d").url).to eq("http://a.com?a=b&c=d")
66:     expect(Story.new(url: "http://a.com?linkId=track").url).to eq("http://a.com")

71: 
72:     expect(Story.new(url: "http://example/").tap(&:valid?).errors[:url]).to_not be_empty
73:     expect(Story.new(url: "ftp://example.com/").tap(&:valid?).errors[:url]).to_not be_empty
74:     expect(Story.new(url: "http://example.com:123/").tap(&:valid?).errors[:url]).to be_empty
75:   end

96:   it "parses domain properly" do

99:       "http://example.com" => "example.com",
100:       "https://example.com" => "example.com",
101:       "http://example.com:8000" => "example.com",
102:       "http://example.com:8000/" => "example.com",
103:       "http://www3.example.com/goose" => "example.com",
104:       "http://flub.example.com" => "flub.example.com",
105:       "http://www10.org" => "www10.org",

123:   it "converts a title to a url slug properly" do
124:     s = create(:story, title: "Hello there, this is a title")
125:     expect(s.title_as_slug).to eq("hello_there_this_is_title")
126: 
127:     s = create(:story, title: "Hello _ underscore")
128:     expect(s.title_as_slug).to eq("hello_underscore")
129:

134:     expect(s.title_as_slug).to eq("one_second_war_what_time_will_you_die")
135:   end

142:     expect(s.is_editable_by_user?(u)).to be false

145:   context "fetching titles" do
146:     let(:story_directory) { Rails.root.join "spec/fixtures/story_pages/" }
147: 
148:     # this is more elaborate than the previous system, because now it needs to know the content type
149:     def fake_response(content, type, code = "200")
150:       res = Net::HTTPResponse.new(1.0, code, "OK")
151:       res.add_field("content-type", type)
152:       # we can't seemingly just set body, so...
153:       allow(res).to receive(:body).and_return(content)
154:       res
155:     end

157:     it "can fetch PDF titles properly" do
158:       content = File.read(story_directory + "titled.pdf")
159:       res = fake_response(content, "application/pdf")
160:       s = build(:story)
161:       s.fetched_response = res
162:       expect(s.fetched_attributes[:title]).to eq("Taking a Long Look at QUIC")
163:     end

166:       content = File.read(story_directory + "title_ampersand.html")
167:       res = fake_response(content, "text/html")
168:       s = build(:story)
169:       s.fetched_response = res
170:       expect(s.fetched_attributes[:title]).to eq("B2G demo & quick hack // by Paul Rouget")
171: 
172:       content = File.read(story_directory + "title_google.html")
173:       res = fake_response(content, "text/html")
174:       s = build(:story)
175:       s.fetched_response = res
176:       expect(s.fetched_attributes[:title]).to eq("Google")
177:     end
178: 
179:     it "does not fetch title with a port specified" do
180:       expect(Sponge).to_not receive(:new)
181:       story = Story.new url: "https://example.com:123/"
182:       expect(story.fetched_attributes[:title]).to eq("")
183:     end
184: 
185:     it "does not follow rel=canonical when this is to the main page" do
186:       url = "https://www.mcsweeneys.net/articles/who-said-it-donald-trump-or-regina-george"
187:       s = build(:story, url: url)
188:       s.fetched_response = File.read(story_directory + "canonical_root.html")
189:       expect(s.fetched_attributes[:url]).to eq(url)
190:     end
191: 
192:     it "does not assign canonical url when the response is non-200" do
193:       url = "https://www.mcsweeneys.net/a/who-said-it-donald-trump-or-regina-george"
194:       content = File.read(story_directory + "canonical_error.html")
195:       res = fake_response(content, "text/html", "404")
196: 
197:       expect_any_instance_of(Sponge)
198:         .to receive(:fetch)
199:         .and_return(Net::HTTPResponse.new(1.0, 404, "OK"))
200: 
201:       s = build(:story, url: url)
202:       s.fetched_response = res
203:       expect(s.fetched_attributes[:url]).to eq(url)
204:     end
205: 
206:     it "assigns canonical when url when it resolves 200" do
207:       url = "https://www.mcsweeneys.net/a/who-said-it-donald-trump-or-regina-george"
208:       canonical = "https://www.mcsweeneys.net/articles/who-said-it-donald-trump-or-regina-george"
209:       content = File.read(story_directory + "canonical_error.html")
210:       res = fake_response(content, "text/html")
211: 
212:       expect_any_instance_of(Sponge)
213:         .to receive(:fetch)
214:         .and_return(Net::HTTPResponse.new(1.0, 200, "OK"))
215: 
216:       s = build(:story, url: url)
217:       s.fetched_response = res
218:       expect(s.fetched_attributes[:url]).to eq(canonical)

23:       s = Sponge.new

25:       s.timeout = 45

27:       if res.present?

29: 
30:         # turn newlines into double newlines, so they become paragraphs
31:         j["text"] = j["text"].to_s.gsub("\n", "\n\n")

33:         while j["text"].include?("\n\n\n")

3: require "commonmarker"
4: 
5: class Markdowner
6:   COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS = {
7:     extension: {
8:       tagfilter: true,
9:       autolink: true,
10:       strikethrough: true,
11:       header_ids: nil,
12:       shortcodes: nil
13:     },
14:     render: {
15:       escape: true,
16:       hardbreaks: false,
17:       escaped_char_spans: false
18:     }
19:   }
20:   USERNAME_MENTION = /\B([@~]#{User::VALID_USERNAME})\b/o
21: 
22:   # Rerender all markdown (#1627) cached in db columns, a pattern that predates Rails frament
23:   # caching and our adoption of the feature (6ce15c4f in 2025-10). As we build confidence in
24:   # fragment caching we should drop these columns.
25:   def self.rerender_db_markdown!
26:     Comment.all.find_each { |c| c.update_columns markeddown_comment: Markdowner.to_html(c.comment, as_of: c.created_at) }
27:     ModNote.all.find_each { |n| n.update_columns markeddown_note: Markdowner.to_html(n.note, as_of: n.created_at) }
28:     Story.where.not(description: "")
29:       .find_each { |s|
30:         s.update_columns markeddown_description: Markdowner.to_html(
31:           s.description,
32:           allow_images: s.can_have_images?,
33:           as_of: s.created_at
34:         )
35:       }
36:   end
37: 
38:   # allow_images: transform ![](example.com/img.jpg) to a hotlinked image; default is to print a link to the URL
39:   # as_of: what timestamp to add @mention links as of, because users rename; default is nil which is overridden
40:   #        to Time.current for newly created models passing their nil created_at
41:   def self.to_html(text, allow_images: false, as_of: nil)
42:     if text.blank?
43:       return ""
44:     end
45: 
46:     as_of ||= Time.current
47: 
48:     root = Commonmarker.parse(text.to_s, options: COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS)
49: 
50:     walk_text_nodes(root) { |n| link_username_mentions(n, as_of:) }
51: 
52:     ng = Nokogiri::HTML(root.to_html(
53:       options: COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS,
54:       plugins: {syntax_highlighter: nil}
55:     ))
56: 
57:     # change <h1>, <h2>, etc. headings to just bold tags
58:     ng.css("h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6").each do |h|
59:       h.name = "strong"
60:     end
61: 
62:     # This should happen before adding rel=ugc to all links
63:     convert_images_to_links(ng) unless allow_images
64: 
65:     # make links have rel=ugc
66:     ng.css("a").each do |h|
67:       h[:rel] = "ugc"
68:     end
69: 
70:     if ng.at_css("body")
71:       ng.at_css("body").inner_html
72:     else
73:       ""
74:     end
75:   end
76: 
77:   def self.to_raw(text)
78:     if text.blank?
79:       return ""
80:     end
81: 
82:     root = Commonmarker.parse(text.to_s.dup.force_encoding("utf-8"), options: COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS)
83: 
84:     root.walk do |node|
85:       if node.type == :image ||
86:           node.type == :link ||
87:           node.type == :emph ||
88:           node.type == :strong ||
89:           node.type == :strikethrough
90:         node.insert_before(node.first_child)
91:         node.delete
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     root.to_commonmark
96:   end
97: 
98:   def self.walk_text_nodes(node, &block)
99:     return if node.type == :link
100:     return block.call(node) if node.type == :text
101: 
102:     node.each do |child|
103:       walk_text_nodes(child, &block)
104:     end
105:   end
106: 
107:   def self.link_username_mentions(node, as_of:)
108:     # This does 1+n queries in username linkification in comments/bios because this works inorder on
109:     # the parse tree rather than running once over the text. Proper fix: loop to find usernames, do
110:     # one lookup, then loop again to manipulate the nodes for usernames that exist.
111: 
112:     while node
113:       return unless node.string_content =~ USERNAME_MENTION
114:       before, user, after = $`, $1, $'
115: 
116:       node.string_content = before
117: 
118:       if Username.where("created_at < ? and (? < renamed_away_at or renamed_away_at is null)", as_of, as_of).exists?(username: user[1..])
119:         link = Commonmarker::Node.new(:link, url: Rails.application.root_url + "~#{user[1..]}")
120:         node.insert_after(link)
121: 
122:         link_text = Commonmarker::Node.new(:text)
123:         link_text.string_content = user
124:         link.append_child(link_text)
125: 
126:         node = link
127:       else
128:         node.string_content += user
129:       end
130: 
131:       if after.length > 0
132:         remainder = Commonmarker::Node.new(:text)
133:         remainder.string_content = after
134:         node.insert_after(remainder)
135: 
136:         node = remainder
137:       else
138:         node = nil
139:       end
140:     end
141:   end
142: 
143:   def self.convert_images_to_links(node)
144:     node.css("img").each do |img|
145:       link = node.create_element("a")
146: 
147:       link["href"], title, alt = img.attributes
148:         .values_at("src", "title", "alt")
149:         .map(&:to_s)
150: 
151:       link.content = [title, alt, link["href"]].find(&:present?)
152: 
153:       img.replace link
154:     end
155:   end
156: end

3: class Utils
4:   # URI.parse is not very lenient, so we can't use it
5:   # requires a . in domain because we don't want people submitting links to internal hostnames
6:   URL_RE = /\A(?<protocol>https?):\/\/(?<domain>(?:[^.\/]+\.)+[a-z-]+)(?<port>:\d+)?(?:\/(?:[\w.~:\/?#\[\]@!$&'()*+,;=%-]*))?\z/i
7: 
8:   # utility that works on stringlikes (for possibly invalid user input + searches)
9:   # If this function changes, you should run Story.refresh_normalized_urls! and Link.refresh_normalized_urls!
10:   def self.normalize(url)
11:     return url if url.blank?
12:     url = url.to_s.dup # in case passed a Url or frozen string
13: 
14:     url.slice! %r{#.*$} # remove anchor
15:     url.slice! %r{/$} # remove trailing slash
16:     url.slice! %r{\.html?$} # remove .htm, .html
17: 
18:     # remove some common "directory index" pages that are commonly served for dirs
19:     url.slice! %r{/index$} # includes index.html? from previous
20:     url.slice! %r{/index\.php}
21:     url.slice! %r{/Default\.aspx$}
22: 
23:     url.slice! %r{https?://} # consider http and https the same
24:     url.sub!(/\Awww\d*\.(.+?\..+)/, '\1') # remove www\d* from domain if the url is not like www10.org
25: 
26:     url, *args = url.split(/[&?]/) # trivia: ?a=1?c=2 is a valid uri
27:     url ||= "" # if original url was just '#', ''.split made url nil
28:     if args.any?
29:       url += "?"
30:       url += args.map { |arg| arg.split("=") }.sort.map { |arg| arg.join("=") }.join("&")
31:     end
32: 
33:     # unify arxiv page and pdf based on their identifier https://arxiv.org/help/arxiv_identifier
34:     url = url.sub %r{^arxiv\.org/(?:abs|html|pdf)/(?<id>\d{4}\.\d{4,5}(?:v\d)?)(?:\.pdf)?}, 'arxiv.org/abs/\k<id>'
35: 
36:     # unify rfc-editor.org pages based on their URL structures:
37:     # https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9338.html
38:     # https://www.rfc-editor.org/info/rfc9338
39:     url = url.sub %r{rfc-editor\.org/(?:rfc|info)/rfc(\d+)[^/]*$}, 'rfc-editor.org/rfc/\1'
40: 
41:     url = url.sub %r{^m\.youtube\.com/}, "youtube.com/"
42:     url = url.sub %r{^youtu\.be/}, "youtube.com/watch?v="
43:     url = url.sub %r{^youtube\.com/.*v=(?<id>[A-Za-z0-9\-_]+).*}, 'youtube.com/watch?v=\k<id>'
44:     url.sub(
45:       %r{^youtube\.com/playlist\?.*list=(?<id>[A-Za-z0-9\-_]+).*},
46:       'youtube.com/playlist?list=\k<id>'
47:     )
48:   end
49: 
50:   def self.random_str(len)
51:     str = ""
52:     while str.length < len
53:       chr = OpenSSL::Random.random_bytes(1)
54:       ord = chr.unpack1("C")
55: 
56:       #          0            9              A            Z              a            z
57:       if ord.between?(48, 57) || ord.between?(65, 90) || ord.between?(97, 122)
58:         str += chr
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     str
63:   end
64: 
65:   def self.silence_stream(*streams)
66:     on_hold = streams.collect(&:dup)
67:     streams.each do |stream|
68:       stream.reopen(File::NULL)
69:       stream.sync = true
70:     end
71:     yield
72:   ensure
73:     streams.each_with_index do |stream, i|
74:       stream.reopen(on_hold[i])
75:     end
76:   end
77: end
